Simulation Systems

Q01
Simulation helps test machine layouts, production schedules, bottlenecks, and downtime or delays. This application refers to _____.
Q02
Simulation helps determine required bandwidth, router capacity, and the best communication protocols. This problem area is _____.
Q03
Simulation is used to estimate CPU speed, memory requirements, and software response time in _____.
Q04
Transportation systems that involve vehicles, passengers, traffic signals, and time constraints are best studied under _____.
Q05
Simulation focusing on customer waiting time, number of servers, and service speed applies to _____.
Q06
Simulation that compares old processes with new processes and identifies delays and redundancies is used in _____.
Q07
Supply chains consisting of suppliers, manufacturers, warehouses, and retailers are analyzed through simulation under _____.
Q08
Simulation that helps minimize shortages and overstocking is used in _____.
Q09
Simulation involving equipment, workers, transportation, and safety concerns applies to _____.
Q10
Which statement BEST describes an advantage of simulation?
Q11
One major benefit of simulation is its ability to _____.
Q12
Which advantage of simulation enables comparison of different outcomes?
Q13
Why does simulation accuracy sometimes become a problem?
Q14

Q15

Q16
Over-simplifying a simulation model may result in _____.
Q17
Which pitfall occurs when simulation output is not properly analyzed?
Q18
Which statement BEST describes a disadvantage of simulation?
Q19
Which option reflects a limitation rather than a benefit of simulation?
Q20
Simulation is MOST appropriate to use when _____.
Q21
Which situation BEST justifies the use of simulation?
Q22
Simulation is useful for testing "what-if" scenarios because it allows _____.
Q23
Why are "what-if" scenarios commonly analyzed using simulation?
Q24
Simulation is preferred when analytical formulas are not used because they _____.
Q25
Which condition MOST strongly indicates the need for simulation?
Q26
Which of the following is NOT a reason to use simulation?
Q27
Simulation is often chosen over direct experimentation primarily to _____.
Q28
When a system is too complex for direct mathematical analysis, simulation helps by _____.
Q29
The MAIN reason simulation is used instead of real experimentation is to _____.
Q30
What refers to the imitation of a real-world process or system over time, usually via a model?
Q31
Which type of system includes randomness where different runs produce different results?
Q32
What type of system produces the same output for the same input every time?
Q33
Using a model to study the behavior of a system is known as:
Q34
What is a simplified representation of a real system?
Q35
A collection of interacting components forming a whole is called a:
Q36
Problems solved using computer models are classified as:
Q37
Problems solved using mathematical formulas are known as:
Q38
Which type of system includes time evolution?
Q39
A system that changes only at specific points in time is called:
Q40
A system that changes smoothly over time is classified as:
Q41
A simulation where state changes occur only at specific events is known as:
Q42
Comparing simulation results with the real system is referred to as:
Q43
Ensuring the logic and code of a simulation work as intended is known as:
Q44
Converting a conceptual model into a computer program is called:
Q45

Q46
Running the simulation and collecting output data belongs to:
Q47
Interpreting findings from simulation results for decision-making is called:
Q48
Documenting assumptions, model details, and results is part of:
Q49

Q50
